title: Add and subtract mixed numbers (with regrouping)
description: Practice adding and subtracting mixed numbers with common (like) denominators. Items require regrouping (borrowing).
topic_path: Module 5: Fraction equivalence, ordering, and operations
Topic F: Addition and subtraction of fractions by decomposition
Add and subtract mixed numbers (with regrouping)

hints:
Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by adding each piece. 

$\blueD5 \maroonD{\dfrac{2}{5}} + \blueD3 \maroonD{\dfrac{4}{5}}$

Our numerator is larger than our denominator so our fraction is greater than $1$. Let's rewrite our improper fraction as a mixed number. 


[[☃ image 1]]

$ \maroonD{\dfrac{6}{5}} =  \blueD1 \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{5}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by adding each piece. 

$\blueD8 \maroonD{\dfrac{3}{4}} + \blueD5 \maroonD{\dfrac{2}{4}}$

Our numerator is larger than our denominator so our fraction is greater than $1$. Let's rewrite our improper fraction as a mixed number. 


[[☃ image 1]]

$ \maroonD{\dfrac54}=\blueD1 \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{4}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by adding each piece. 

$\blueD3 \maroonD{\dfrac{10}{12}} + \blueD4 \maroonD{\dfrac{7}{12}}$

Our numerator is larger than our denominator so our fraction is greater than $1$. Let's rewrite our improper fraction as a mixed number. 


[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD{\dfrac{17}{12}}=\blueD1 \maroonD{\dfrac{5}{12}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by adding each piece. 

$\blueD5 \maroonD{\dfrac{2}{3}} + \blueD7 \maroonD{\dfrac{2}{3}}$

Our numerator is larger than our denominator so our fraction is greater than $1$. Let's rewrite our improper fraction as a mixed number. 


[[☃ image 1]]

$ \maroonD{\dfrac{4}{3}}=\blueD1 \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{3}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by adding each piece. 

$\blueD8 \maroonD{\dfrac{2}{6}} + \blueD2 \maroonD{\dfrac{5}{6}}$

Our numerator is larger than our denominator so our fraction is greater than $1$. Let's rewrite our improper fraction as a mixed number. 



[[☃ image 1]]

$ \maroonD{\dfrac{7}{6}}=\blueD1 \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{6}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by adding each piece. 

$\blueD7 \maroonD{\dfrac{4}{8}} + \blueD2 \maroonD{\dfrac{7}{8}}$

Our numerator is larger than our denominator so our fraction is greater than $1$. Let's rewrite our improper fraction as a mixed number. 


[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD{\dfrac{11}{8}}=\blueD1 \maroonD{\dfrac{3}{8}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by adding each piece. 

$\blueD4 \maroonD{\dfrac{7}{10}} + \blueD5 \maroonD{\dfrac{6}{10}}$

Our numerator is larger than our denominator so our fraction is greater than $1$. Let's rewrite our improper fraction as a mixed number. 


[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD{\dfrac{13}{10}}=\blueD1 \maroonD{\dfrac{3}{10}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by subtracting each piece. 

$\blueD4 \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{4}} - \blueD2 \maroonD{\dfrac{2}{4}}$

First, let's subtract the $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{4} &\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{4}}\\\\
-&\blueD{2}&\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{4}}\\
\hline{}\\
\end{align}$

We cannot subtract $\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{4}} -\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{4}}$ because $\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{4}}$ is **less** than $\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{4}}$.

We need to regroup $\blueD4\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{4}}$ so that the fractional part is **greater** than $\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{4}}$.



[[☃ image 1]]



$\blueD4 \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{4}} =\blueD3 \maroonD{\dfrac{5}{4}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by subtracting each piece. 

$\blueD8 \maroonD{\dfrac{2}{4}} - \blueD1 \maroonD{\dfrac{3}{4}}$

First, let's subtract the $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.


$\begin{align}
&\blueD{8} &\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{4}}\\\\
-&\blueD{1}&\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{4}}\\
\hline{}\\
\end{align}$

We cannot subtract $\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{4}} - \maroonD{\dfrac{3}{4}}$ because $\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{4}}$ is **less** than $\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{4}}$.

We need to regroup $\blueD8\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{4}}$ so that the fractional part is **greater** than $\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{4}}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align} \blueD8\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{4}}&=\blueD7+\maroonD{1}+ \maroonD{\dfrac{2}{4}}\\\\
&=\blueD7+ \maroonD{\dfrac44}+\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{4}}\\\\
&=\blueD7 \maroonD{\dfrac{6}{4}}
\end{align}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by subtracting each piece. 

$\blueD5\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{5}} - \blueD3 \maroonD{\dfrac{4}{5}}$

First, let's subtract the $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.


$\begin{align}
&\blueD{5} &\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{5}}\\\\
-&\blueD{3}&\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{5}}\\
\hline{}\\
\end{align}$

We cannot subtract $\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{5}} - \maroonD{\dfrac{4}{5}}$ because $\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{5}}$ is **less** than $\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{5}}$.

We need to regroup $\blueD5\maroonD{ \dfrac{2}{5}}$ so that the fractional part is **greater** than $\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{5}}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align} \blueD5\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{5}}&=\blueD4+\maroonD{1}+ \maroonD{\dfrac{2}{5}}\\\\
&=\blueD4+ \maroonD{\dfrac55}+\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{5}}\\\\
&=\blueD4 \maroonD{\dfrac{7}{5}}
\end{align}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by subtracting each piece. 

$\blueD7 \maroonD{\dfrac{4}{6}} - \blueD5 \maroonD{\dfrac{5}{6}}$

First, let's subtract the $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.


$\begin{align}
&\blueD{7} &\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{6}}\\\\
-&\blueD{5}&\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{6}}\\
\hline{}\\
\end{align}$

We cannot subtract $\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{6}} - \maroonD{\dfrac{5}{6}}$ because $\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{6}}$ is **less** than $\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{6}}$.

We need to regroup $\blueD7\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{6}}$ so that the fractional part is **greater** than $\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{6}}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align} \blueD7\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{6}}&=\blueD6+\maroonD{1}+ \maroonD{\dfrac{4}{6}}\\\\
&=\blueD6+ \maroonD{\dfrac66}+\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{6}}\\\\
&=\blueD6 \maroonD{\dfrac{10}{6}}
\end{align}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by subtracting each piece. 

$\blueD5 \maroonD{\dfrac{4}{8}} - \blueD3 \maroonD{\dfrac{7}{8}}$
First, let's subtract the $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.


$\begin{align}
&\blueD{5} &\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{8}}\\\\
-&\blueD{3}&\maroonD{\dfrac{7}{8}}\\
\hline{}\\
\end{align}$

We cannot subtract $\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{8}} - \maroonD{\dfrac{7}{8}}$ because $\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{8}}$ is **less** than $\maroonD{\dfrac{7}{8}}$.
We need to regroup $\blueD5\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{8}}$ so that the fractional part is **greater** than $\maroonD{\dfrac{7}{8}}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align} \blueD5\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{8}}&=\blueD4+\maroonD{1}+ \maroonD{\dfrac{4}{8}}\\\\
&=\blueD4+ \maroonD{\dfrac88}+\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{8}}\\\\
&=\blueD4 \maroonD{\dfrac{12}{8}}
\end{align}$
We can replace $\blueD5 \maroonD{\dfrac{4}{8}}$ with $\blueD4 \maroonD{\dfrac{12}{8}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\overset{\blueD4}{\cancel{\blueD5}} &\maroonD{\dfrac{\overset{12}{\cancel{4}}}{8}}\\\\
-&\,\ \blueD{3}&\maroonD{\dfrac{7}{8}}\\
\hline{}
\end{align}$
Now, let's subtract the $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\overset{\blueD4}{\cancel{\blueD5}} &\maroonD{\dfrac{\overset{12}{\cancel{4}}}{8}}\\\\
-&\,\ \blueD{3}&\maroonD{\dfrac{7}{8}}\\
\hline{}
&&\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{8}}\\
\end{align}$
Next, let's subtract the $\blueD{\text{whole numbers}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\overset{\blueD4}{\cancel{\blueD5}} &\maroonD{\dfrac{\overset{12}{\cancel{4}}}{8}}\\\\
-&\,\ \blueD{3}&\maroonD{\dfrac{7}{8}}\\
\hline{}
&\,\ \blueD1&\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{8}}\\
\end{align}$
${1 \dfrac{5}{8}}={5 \dfrac{4}{8}} - {3\dfrac{7}{8}} $

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by subtracting each piece. 

$\blueD{6} \maroonD{\dfrac{6}{10}} - \blueD3\maroonD{\dfrac{7}{10}}$

First, let's subtract the $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.


$\begin{align}
&\blueD{6} &\maroonD{\dfrac{6}{10}}\\\\
-&\blueD{3}&\maroonD{\dfrac{7}{10}}\\
\hline{}\\
\end{align}$

We cannot subtract $\maroonD{\dfrac{6}{10}} - \maroonD{\dfrac{7}{10}}$ because $\maroonD{\dfrac{6}{10}}$ is **less** than $\maroonD{\dfrac{7}{10}}$.

We need to regroup $\blueD{6}\maroonD{\dfrac{6}{10}}$ so that the fractional part is **greater** than $\maroonD{\dfrac{7}{10}}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align} \blueD6\maroonD{\dfrac{6}{10}}&=\blueD5+\maroonD{1}+ \maroonD{\dfrac{6}{10}}\\\\
&=\blueD5+ \maroonD{\dfrac{10}{10}}+\maroonD{\dfrac{6}{10}}\\\\
&=\blueD5 \maroonD{\dfrac{16}{10}}
\end{align}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by subtracting each piece. 

$\blueD8 \maroonD{\dfrac{2}{8}} - \blueD3 \maroonD{\dfrac{3}{8}}$

First, let's subtract the $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.


$\begin{align}
&\blueD{8} &\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{8}}\\\\
-&\blueD{3}&\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{8}}\\
\hline\end{align}$

We cannot subtract $\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{8}} - \maroonD{\dfrac{3}{8}}$ because $\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{8}}$ is **less** than $\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{8}}$.

We need to regroup $\blueD8\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{8}}$ so that the fractional part is **greater** than $\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{8}}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align} \blueD8\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{8}}&=\blueD7+\maroonD{1}+ \maroonD{\dfrac{2}{8}}\\\\
&=\blueD7+ \maroonD{\dfrac{8}{8}}+\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{8}}\\\\
&=\blueD7 \maroonD{\dfrac{10}{8}}
\end{align}$
